description: Nightmare Morphus table (Dark Designs pp. 41–43). Dreamstream-haunted forms; may select Bloodbath or Mark of the Nightbane elite Talents as with Stigmata.

entries.name: Floating
entries.description: As a response to nightmares of falling, the Nightbane floats in midair. The Morphus is that of a person who looks to be in a constant state of fright, his face contorted into a grimace or scream, and his hair and clothing fluttering in the wind whether there is an actual wind or not.
customOneOffs: The character is limited to a floating height of 1-3 feet (0.3 to 0.9 m) off the ground.
He cannot fly but rather hovers and floats about. Maximum Spd is equal to his Spd attribute.

Dropping/falling from great heights causes the character to slow down and float at 1-3 feet above the ground, taking no damage from falls. However, impact from being struck by a vehicle or weapon does full damage.
Hovering does not leave footprints/tracks and prevents setting off pressure plates, but he remains vulnerable to motion and heat detection.
The character has a Fear of Heights phobia plus two other phobias or random insanities.

entries.name: Contorted Body
entries.description: The body of this Nightbane is twisted far out of normal shape. The spine might be twisted so that the torso faces the opposite way, hunched over, or doubled backward so that the legs straddle the abdomen, and/or curled forward with the legs wrapping around the head.
customOneOffs: The character might waddle, crab-walk, crawl, or even tumble to get around, but may do so with surprising speed and agility given the deformities present.

entries.name: Gnarled Limbs
entries.description: Arms, legs, and any other limbs of the Morphus are deformed. The arms and legs might be juxtaposed, elongated, or marred by odd knots, grooves, and holes.
customOneOffs: The Nightbane is double-jointed in all of his limbs.

entries.name: Spines
entries.description: The Nightbane has 1D4+2 articulated spines protruding from the sides of his upper torso in addition to his arms and legs. Made of bone or metal and ending in very sharp points, they are suitable as close-quarters weapons.

appearanceConstraints.customClothingNote: Spines must be taped or otherwise strapped down for disguises.

customOneOffs: Spines have a reach equal to that of the Nightbane's arms (roughly 3 feet/0.9 m on average).
The character can attack with one or two spines in a single melee attack (roll a single strike even if using two spines).
Averages 4 spines (1D4+2). Each has its own S.D.C. pool and will completely regrow in an hour if destroyed.

entries.name: Pulsating Skin
entries.description: The skin of the Morphus is an unnatural color, or pale and mottled with dark patches of blue and purplish spider-webbed veins. The skin moves, ripples, crawls, and/or pulsates as if there is something alive and moving beneath it.

combatContextModifiers.note: Horror Factor must be rolled any time a new opponent wishes to grapple. Failure means the attacker hesitates, loses one melee attack and initiative, and engages with -1 to all combat maneuvers (strike, parry, dodge) because they are distracted and grossed out.

customOneOffs: The pulsating/squirming sensation is strongly felt when holding or grappling the Nightbane.

entries.name: Straight-Jacket
entries.description: The Nightbane is wrapped in what looks like a straight-jacket. The long sleeves and body of the jacket are fitted with belts and fasteners that restrict the movement of the arms.
customOneOffs: Body Block/Ram counts as two attacks to perform.
The text lists +2 to P.P. in the bonuses and -2 to P.P. in the penalties (net 0).
A leather or cage-like mask may also be part of the Morphus, obscuring the face and acting as something of a muzzle to prevent biting.
The arms are immobilized, so the Nightbane is unable to use tools and weapons, cannot parry attacks, nor perform skills that require hands and arms (like Climbing).
If the character has the Swimming skill, he is able to tread water with the legs to stay afloat.
Enables him to run along rooftops, walls, ledges, and taught ropes and cables like a skilled tightrope walker and do somersaults and rolls.

entries.name: Torture Mask
entries.description: The face and head of the Morphus form is covered by a mask intended as a torture device. It is part of the Morphus form and cannot be removed.
customOneOffs: Interrogators find their Interrogation skill to be -30% when questioning this character.
The Nightbane is somehow able to see, breathe and speak through the mask as if it was not there.

entries.name: Massive Maw
entries.description: The mouth of the Nightbane becomes a giant, devouring maw unlike anything from the natural world.

combatContextModifiers.note: The character can latch onto an opponent after a successful bite attack like being caught in the jaws of a crocodile. The target needs a Supernatural P.S. twice that of the Nightbane to pry free.

customOneOffs: Massive Maw bite: only P.P. bonuses can be added to this attack's strike roll.
Impervious to spoiled food; can eat raw and rotting meat and foods without getting ill.
Can gnaw through a wood fence, crate, door or other barrier in 1D4+2 melee rounds.
Speech is much more difficult, imposing a -20% on all Language skills. Strangely enough, this does not seem to hamper spell casting.

entries.name: Gruesome Weapon
entries.description: Either held in the hand or grafted to one or more limbs is a nightmarish weapon. Such weapons are usually rusty, dripping blood or covered in gore or slime, and/or jagged/serrated or made from bone.
customOneOffs: +2 to pull punch with the Gruesome Weapon.
Damage is equal to the 'Bane's Supernatural P.S. +3D6 damage regardless of the type or size of the weapon.
If two smaller weapons are used instead of one large one, the +3D6 damage is divided as +1D6+3 for each.
Automatically has W.P. Paired Weapons skill.
If the S.D.C. of the Gruesome weapon is reduced to zero, it crumbles to dust and does not reappear until all of the character's personal S.D.C. are restored to full capacity.
Without this weapon, the Nightbane's other combat bonuses are all reduced by half.
Other weapons can be used but are -2 to strike and parry.

entries.name: Other
entries.description: Random roll or choose another table and relate the result to the character's fears or nightmares.
customOneOffs: The most obvious choices include Stigmata, Pestilence, or Animal tables. Otherwise, re-roll on the Nightmare table and ignore this result.
